window: last 365 days (live refresh plus a few older notable items)
note: Merged dataset (2026-06-26): the original Hacker News sweep plus a live refresh that added new HN items and the official Together AI status page. Reddit, Trustpilot, and G2 blocked automated requests (HTTP 403) and were not included. Most public HN mentions of Together AI were neutral or positive (cheap pricing, wide model support, ZDR/privacy); the items below are the genuine negative-leaning complaints found. BIAS FLAGS: tg-0003 (jadendhm) and tg-0009 (booty) were posted by people launching competing routing products (ApeKey), and tg-0004 (vshah1016) is a competitor benchmark - treat their comparative claims accordingly.

complaints.complaint: A developer reported that Together AI 'lacked some key features like prompt history, parameter adjustments, and the ability to compare different prompt results and costs directly' for prompt engineering (said while launching a tool built on top of it).
complaints.quote: We tried using Together AI, but found it lacked some key features like prompt history, parameter adjustments, and the ability to compare different prompt results and costs directly.

complaints.complaint: In a user's informal latency benchmark, Together AI was the slowest of three providers tested (2.60s vs. Fireworks 1.42s and Groq 1.28s) for a Llama-3-70B request.
complaints.quote: Time taken for get_together_ai_response_requests: 2.60 seconds ... Groq is still faster 1.28s vs 1.42s for fireworks

complaints.complaint: A developer building a multi-provider routing product characterized Together AI as 'cheap but slower' relative to Groq and Fireworks, and cited surprise end-of-month bills from juggling several pay-as-you-go providers.
complaints.quote: Groq is fast but only has a few models, Together AI is cheap but slower, Fireworks is reliable but more expensive.

complaints.complaint: A competing provider's benchmark claimed higher throughput than Together AI on the same VLM workload (588 tok/s vs. Together AI's 298 tok/s), implying lower throughput on that workload. Source is a competitor launch, so treat as biased/comparative.
complaints.quote: On multimodal pipelines we get better performance than big players (588 tok/s vs. Together AI's 298 on the same VLM workload).

complaints.complaint: A commenter argued Together AI's low token prices are 'heavily subsidized by more than $1B in VC money,' raising sustainability/true-cost concerns rather than a service defect.
complaints.quote: Even that $1/Mtok provided by Together AI is heavily subsidized by more than $1B in VC money. This makes it unclear how the true cost curve is progressing.

complaints.complaint: A 'Tell HN' post alleged Together AI is involved in a proof-of-work GPU 'mining' scheme (via a $PRL/Pearl partnership) the author calls 'snake oil and vaporware.' This is an unverified accusation/opinion, not a confirmed fact.
complaints.quote: There is circumstantial evidence that, among others, NVIDIA and Together AI are behind it ... They are running useless PoW in an AI trench coat.

complaints.complaint: A commenter said that while Together AI is a good US provider, its pricing is roughly 15x more expensive than DeepSeek's subsidized Chinese pricing, making it unattractive at that price point for some workloads.
complaints.quote: Together.ai is a good US provider but then it's 15X more expensive than DeepSeek's Chinese-subsidized pricing. It's really not that attractive at that price point.

complaints.complaint: A user reported that Together AI seemed unreliable, citing frequent outages or higher-than-normal latency. The official status page corroborates sub-100% 60-day uptime on several inference models over the same period.
complaints.quote: tangential note about together.ai, they seem fairly unreliable with constant outages or higher than normal latency.

complaints.complaint: A commenter raised sustainability/true-cost concerns about open-model inference providers (naming Together AI's pricing page), arguing the low headline token prices may be possible only because vendors are burning through investor cash.
complaints.quote: it's possible that they are burning through investor cash as well, and apples-to-apples comparisons are not possible.

complaints.complaint: The official Together AI status page shows reduced 60-day uptime on several inference models as of this snapshot - notably Google Gemma 4 31B IT at 97.869%, with GLM 5.2 (99.101%), Qwen3.5 235B (99.227%), and DeepSeek V4 Pro (99.587%) also below their peers - indicating intermittent reliability dips even though all services currently read 'operational.'
complaints.quote: Google Gemma 4 31B IT 97.869% uptime, 60 days.

complaints.complaint: Auto-collected Hacker News mention referencing Together AI: "500+ open-source and frontier AI models with unified billing and super fast inference. Also way cheaper than other tool like Openrouter and Together AI. Status: Beta - accepting pilots for free API credits Reference: gatewayz.ai What's everyone else cooking? Let's hear it I'm curious"
complaints.quote: 500+ open-source and frontier AI models with unified billing and super fast inference. Also way cheaper than other tool like Openrouter and Together AI. Status: Beta - accepting pilots for free API credits Reference: gatewayz.ai What's everyone else cooking? Let's hear it I'm curious
